query: Replay QA tells you what is broken before your users do alternative

results.title: Replay QA — AI wrote the app. Replay QA finds what broke.
results.content:
The result: AI-built apps ship without meaningful test coverage. Internal tools get deployed with no QA layer at all. And the bugs your users hit are the first signal that something is wrong.

Replay QA gives AI-built apps the QA loop they're missing.

Two ways to run it

## Add your GitHub repo, or drop in a URL

Same autonomous QA, two ways to start — a continuous quality gate for teams, or a one-time check for whatever you just shipped.

Continuously-running

### Connect your GitHub repo

A quality gate that stays on as your team ships. Add your repo, authenticate with GitHub, and the Replay QA app installs — no test suite, no config. Then choose when it runs. [...] Learn more about how Replay QA works

Also available

## Use Replay QA wherever AI is shipping code

### Replay for CI

Already running Playwright or Cypress? Replay QA records every test run, analyzes every failure, and posts root cause and fix to your PR automatically.

Learn more →

### Replay QA API

Building a software factory or AI coding platform? Embed Replay QA as a quality gate via API. Every app your system generates gets tested before it ships.

Talk to us →

## Built on technology teams already trust

Replay QA runs on the same recording engine used every day by teams at Vercel, Glide, Pantheon, and more.

> “Before Replay, we spent somewhere between 1–2 hours per day per dev in this reproducibility purgatory.”

Mark Probst

VP Engineering, Glide [...] Mark Probst

VP Engineering, Glide

> “The tools that we were using before were barely better than useless. You'd say thanks for the console log screenshots and spend 2–3 days trying to recreate the issues.”

Front End Lead, Pantheon

> “Next.js App Router is now stable in 13.4. It wouldn't have been possible without Replay, we investigated so many super complicated bugs.”

Co-author of Next.js, Vercel

React Maintainer

> “After seeing what Replay can do, I'm setting up a lot more Playwright tests on my PRs. I was afraid to before, due to the extra work of debugging failed tests.”

Ben Ruckman

Co-founder, Getmallow.com

results.title: How Replay QA Works
results.content:
02

### Authenticate with GitHub

Sign in with your GitHub account. One technical lead can complete setup — no procurement, no engineering ticket.

03

### The Replay QA app installs

The GitHub app installs on your repo. No existing Playwright suite, no playwright.config.ts changes, no separate bot to wire up.

04

### Choose your run cadence

Run on every main-branch update, on every pull request, or both. On PRs, root cause and a suggested fix are posted right on the pull request.

Connect your GitHub repo

What you get

## Root cause. Suggested fix. Full recording.

Every bug Replay QA finds comes with everything your agent needs to fix it — not just “something broke.”

Bug found

confidence: high

Checkout button does nothing on mobile Safari

Root cause [...] ## Underneath it all: our time-travel debugger.

Whether you're running Replay QA on a web app or analyzing a CI failure, the same recording engine is at work. Replay captures the full browser runtime deterministically — making AI analysis possible where it wasn't before.

Render chainsWhich component re-rendered, what triggered it, what changed

State flowRedux actions, React state updates, context changes across the component tree

Network timingEvery request and response, with exact payload and timing data

JS executionEvery function call on every frame, with arguments and return values

DOM mutationsWhat changed in the DOM, when, and what code caused it

This isn't guessing from error messages. It's reading the actual execution.

## Test your app for free. [...] How it works

# From URL to bug report. Automatically.

Give Replay QA a URL. It explores your app, records every session with our time-travel debugger, and delivers a root cause and suggested fix for every bug it finds.

How it works

## From URL to bug report — automatically

Testing continuously

## Connect your GitHub repo

The URL drop is a run-it-once check. When you want a quality gate that stays on as your team ships, connect a repo instead — same autonomous QA, running on every change. No test suite, no pipeline config.

01

### Add your repo URL

Paste a link to your GitHub repository — the same “paste a link to your thing” pattern as the URL drop.

02

### Authenticate with GitHub

results.content:

Image 27: Rated 1 out of 5 stars

## Their return system failed me, then they billed me for it
I placed an order with Replay and never received an order confirmation email. When I tried to return the item through their portal, it rejected my email and order number - presumably because of the missing order confirmation. Before I could do anything, their system had already automatically registered my return as "self-arranged," locking me out of the free prepaid return option they advertise. [...] Replay advertises free first returns. When their own system prevents you from using it, they will not honour it. Be warned: if anything goes wrong technically, you will be held responsible for their system's mistakes.

## Sold a completely misleading Black…
Sold a completely misleading Black Friday online deal.

I purchased tems thinking they were part of the deal, so when the message popped up at purchase stage there was no indication of the what the customs charge would be (is it £1 or £100 or £1000?). Response from Replay to say that it's 'out of their control'. 

Upon delivery I paid a customs charge that was almost exactly the same value as the black friday 'discount', so i effective bought full price items.

results.title: What is Replay Testing | Milestone
results.content:
All Glossary

3 min read

# Replay Testing

Software testing is an essential aspect of software development. It is vital to ensure that applications function as expected and remain reliable across updates and changes.

Replay testing stands out among testing methodologies for its efficacy and precision. This article explores replay testing’s intricacies, significance, and contribution to robust software quality.

## What is Replay Testing?

Replay testing is a testing technique in which a user’s interaction with a software application is recorded. It is later replayed to verify that the application behaves as expected.

To implement this approach, you must: [...] Capturing and replaying user interactions helps identify defects early and ensures that software applications remain reliable and robust across updates.

Despite its challenges, replay testing can significantly contribute to the overall quality of software products with proper implementation and maintenance.

Share post on

## Ready to Transform Your GenAI Investments?

Don’t leave your GenAI adoption to chance. With Milestone, you can achieve measurable ROI and maintain a competitive edge.

Book a demo [...] 3. Validation: During playback, the testing tool will compare the software’s current activity to the responses and actions recorded during the recording stage. If any deviations are identified, they are marked as possible defects, and testers can analyze these discrepancies to confirm if genuine bugs exist or if changes are expected due to code changes.
